column5: • Your telephony state shows the default data APN is carrier-controlled and marked CARRIER_EDITED, not user-edited.
• In Android telephony terms, CARRIER_EDITED means the APN record in use is carrier-managed or carrier-modified.
• For example, VZWINTERNET comes up with valid
• the default APN in use is VZWINTERNET and marked CARRIER_EDITED
• The active default APN is VZWINTERNET, and the telephony stack labels it CARRIER_EDITED, indicating the in-use default data APN is carrier-managed rather than obviously subscriber-modified.
• The active default data APN (VZWINTERNET) is shown by the Android telephony stack as CARRIER_EDITED, while the IMS and CBS/MMS APNs are shown as UNEDITED.
• the active default APN is carrier-managed (CARRIER_EDITED)

I need help with a provisioning issue on my Verizon line. My data connection works only partially, and the same problem follows me everywhere I go. My phone only has one APN, VZWINTERNET, and it’s missing the other Verizon APNs that are normally installed automatically on a postpaid 5G line, including the IMS APN. My MMSC field is empty, and the APN roaming protocol is stuck on IPv4 even though Verizon assigns me a full IPv6 address. This mismatch is causing data stalls, webpage failures, and timeouts. I need a full teardown and rebuild of my line’s provisioning on Verizon’s side, including a reset of the IMS profile, a complete APN bundle push, and a refresh of my VoLTE, VoNR, and 5G capabilities. Standard reprovisioning has already been tried and did not fix the problem, so this needs to be escalated.
When I checked my APN settings, I found that my device only has a single APN
column6: VZWINTERNET. On a modern Verizon postpaid 5G line, my device should also have the IMS APN, the administrative APN, and the secondary VZWINTERNET APN that Verizon normally installs through the carrier configuration system. My phone does not receive any of those, and I am unable to add them manually because the menu is locked at the carrier level. The MMSC field is empty, and the MMS proxy and MMS port fields are also empty. Those should be automatically filled on a properly provisioned Verizon line.
